q: A CI/CD pipeline runs three Claude Code steps in sequence: (1) generate a changelog from git commits, (2) review the changelog for accuracy, and (3) check for breaking changes. The team notices that step 2 never flags inaccuracies and step 3 misses obvious breaking changes that the changelog omits. What is the root cause?
opts: The three steps are sharing session context, so steps 2 and 3 inherit the reasoning from step 1 and do not independently evaluate the changelog; each step should run as an independent session
The CLAUDE.md file does not contain changelog formatting standards, so the review step has no criteria to evaluate against
The -p flag is not being used, causing each step to wait for interactive input
The steps need to use --output-format json so that each step can parse the previous step's structured output
rat: Se as três etapas compartilham a mesma sessão, o revisor herda o raciocínio de quem escreveu — não há avaliação independente (viés de auto-confirmação). Cada etapa do pipeline deve rodar como sessão isolada, recebendo apenas o artefato a avaliar.

q: A CI/CD system prompt defines two review categories with the instructions 'Check for security vulnerabilities in each function' and 'Check for performance issues in each loop'. The model frequently calls performance_check for security issues found inside loops, and security_check for performance issues in security-sensitive functions. What is the root cause and best fix?
opts: The model is confused because loops can have both security and performance issues; add a rule that security always takes priority over performance

Keyword overlap between instruction phrasing and tool names causes the model to associate 'loop' with performance and 'function' with security regardless of the actual issue; rewrite instructions and tool descriptions to use distinct, non-overlapping terminology
Add more detailed tool descriptions explaining exactly when each tool should be called
Force tool_choice to 'auto' and let the model determine the correct tool based on the content of each finding
rat: O modelo associou 'loop'→performance e 'function'→security pela sobreposição de palavras-chave entre instruções e nomes de tools, ignorando a natureza real do problema. A correção é reescrever instruções e descrições com terminologia distinta e sem sobreposição.

q: A codebase has test files co-located with source files throughout 50+ directories (e.g., Button.test.tsx next to Button.tsx). The team wants all tests to follow the same conventions regardless of location. What is the most maintainable approach?
opts: Create a rule file in .claude/rules/ with YAML frontmatter paths: ["**/*.test.tsx", "**/*.test.ts"] containing the test conventions
Place a CLAUDE.md file in every directory containing test files
Add all test conventions to the root CLAUDE.md file
Create a skill in .claude/skills/ with the test conventions
rat: Convenções ligadas a padrões de arquivo espalhados pelo repo: .claude/rules/ com frontmatter paths ['**/*.test.tsx', '**/*.test.ts'] — carrega as regras exatamente quando arquivos de teste são tocados. CLAUDE.md em cada diretório (B) é inviável de manter em 50+ pastas.

q: A coordinator agent receives a request to extract a payment processing module from the monolith into a standalone microservice. The task involves creating new API endpoints, migrating database tables, updating 40+ call sites, and writing integration tests. A junior developer suggests the coordinator should handle the entire task itself to avoid subagent communication overhead. Why is this approach wrong?
opts: The coordinator should never write code - it should only route tasks
Loading 40+ files into one context dilutes attention - the coordinator should delegate to scoped subagents instead
The coordinator cannot access file system tools, so it physically cannot make code changes
The coordinator's API rate limits would be exceeded when processing 40+ files
rat: Carregar 40+ arquivos num único contexto dilui a atenção e degrada a qualidade. O coordenador deve decompor e delegar a subagentes com contexto escopado (endpoints, migração de banco, call sites, testes). O 'overhead de comunicação' custa menos que a degradação.

q: A coordinator agent should spawn specialist subagents for billing disputes and technical issues. Its system prompt describes the delegation workflow correctly, yet when it tries to invoke a subagent nothing happens: no subagent is created and no related error is thrown. What is the most likely cause?
opts: The coordinator's allowedTools list does not include 'Task' (the subagent-spawning tool), so it cannot invoke the mechanism for spawning subagents
The subagent's AgentDefinition is missing a description field, preventing it from being invoked
The coordinator needs to pass the full conversation history to the subagent for it to initialise properly
The subagents need to be pre-registered in a configuration file before the coordinator can invoke them
rat: Subagentes são criados via ferramenta Task. Se 'Task' não está no allowedTools do coordenador, o mecanismo de spawn simplesmente não existe para ele — nada acontece e não há erro relacionado. O prompt descrever a delegação não concede a capacidade.

q: A coordinator delegates a large refactoring subtask to a specialist subagent, and the architect must write the subagent's prompt. Which prompt design best follows the guide's guidance on how a coordinator should instruct a subagent?
opts: State the goal and its quality criteria (for example: extract the payment module into a standalone service with no breaking changes to public APIs, and all existing tests must pass) and let the subagent choose how to get there.
Give an exact step-by-step procedure (open file X, change line 12, run command Y, then edit file Z) so the subagent cannot deviate.
Provide only the high-level goal with no success criteria, so the subagent has maximum freedom.
Omit the goal and instead list the tools the subagent may use, letting it infer the objective from the toolset.
rat: O coordenador deve instruir subagentes por objetivo + critérios de qualidade (extrair o módulo sem quebrar APIs públicas, testes passando) e deixar o 'como' com o especialista. Micro-gerenciar passos (B) desperdiça a capacidade do subagente; sem critérios (C) não há definição de sucesso.

q: A coordinator delegates to a data collection agent (web_search, database_query) and an analysis agent (calculate, chart_generate). In testing, the analysis agent calls web_search directly to fetch extra context, bypassing the data collection agent. What is the architectural violation and how should it be fixed?
opts: The analysis agent's allowedTools list includes web_search, which should be removed to enforce scoped tool access. Only the data collection agent should have web_search.
Add a system prompt instruction telling the analysis agent not to use web_search even though it has access
Allow the analysis agent to keep web_search access since it sometimes needs additional context for better analysis
Implement a PostToolUse hook that logs when the analysis agent uses web_search for monitoring purposes
rat: É violação de escopo de ferramentas: se o agente de análise consegue chamar web_search, seu allowedTools está largo demais. A correção é remover a tool — enforcement estrutural. Instrução de prompt (B) não garante; logging (D) só observa.

q: A customer raises three separate issues during one session: a refund inquiry (turns 1-15), a subscription question (turns 16-30), and a payment method update (turns 31-45). At turn 48, the customer asks What happened with my refund? The conversation is approaching context limits. What strategy best maintains the agent's ability to address all issues throughout the session?
opts: Extract and persist structured issue data (order IDs, amounts, statuses) into a separate context layer.
Rely on MCP tools to re-fetch relevant information on demand when the customer references earlier issues.
Summarize earlier turns into a narrative description, preserving full message history only for the active issue.
Implement sliding window context that retains the most recent 30 turns.
rat: Persistir dados estruturados por issue (IDs, valores, status) em camada separada de contexto segue o padrão de 'structured note-taking' recomendado pela Anthropic: fatos críticos sobrevivem à compactação com precisão. Sumarização narrativa (C) é defensável, mas pode perder detalhes exatos como IDs e valores; re-fetch via tools (B) depende de os dados existirem no backend; janela deslizante (D) descarta as issues antigas justamente quando o cliente as retoma.

q: A customer returns 4 hours after their initial session about the same billing dispute. The previous 32-turn session contains lookup_order results showing "Status: PENDING, Expected resolution: 24-48 hours." In testing, you observe that when resuming sessions with stale tool results, the agent often references the outdated data in responses even after subsequent fresh tool calls return different information. What approach most reliably handles returning customers?
opts: Resume with full history but filter out previous `tool_result` messages before resuming, keeping only the human/assistant turns so the agent must re-fetch needed data.
Start a new session, inject a structured summary of the previous interaction (issue type, actions taken, resolution status), then make fresh tool calls before engaging.
Resume with full history and add a system prompt instruction telling the agent to always prefer the most recent tool results when multiple calls to the same tool exist in context.
Resume with full history and configure the agent to automatically re-call all previously-used tools at session start to ensure data freshness.
rat: Iniciar sessão nova com resumo estruturado + chamadas de ferramenta frescas elimina os tool results obsoletos da raiz do problema, preservando a continuidade. Manter histórico completo (A, C, D) mantém ou reintroduz dados velhos no contexto — o próprio enunciado diz que o agente os referencia mesmo após chamadas novas, então instrução de preferência (C) é insuficiente e re-chamar tudo (D) não remove os resultados antigos.

q: A customer says: 'This is ridiculous, I have been waiting 20 minutes. Just connect me to a real person.' The agent has access to the customer's account and can see the issue is a simple password reset that takes 30 seconds to resolve. What should the agent do?
opts: Acknowledge the frustration and offer to resolve the password reset, explaining it will take only 30 seconds - the issue is straightforward and escalation is unnecessary.
Escalate to a human agent immediately without attempting to resolve the issue, because the customer explicitly requested a human.
Run sentiment analysis to confirm the customer is genuinely frustrated before deciding whether to escalate or attempt resolution.
Ask the customer if they would prefer to wait for a human or let the agent resolve the password reset quickly.
rat: O cliente pediu explicitamente um humano após 20 minutos de frustração — respeitar a autonomia do usuário exige escalar imediatamente. Insistir em resolver (A/D) ignora o pedido explícito e tende a aumentar a frustração, mesmo que a solução técnica seja trivial.

q: A customer sends: "This is frustrating. I've explained my issue twice and nothing is being resolved. I want to talk to a real person NOW." The agent has not yet called any tools to investigate their account. What should the agent do?
opts: Acknowledge the frustration and ask one targeted question to understand the specific issue before escalating.
Briefly explain what the agent can help with and offer to resolve the issue quickly, escalating only if the customer repeats their request.
Immediately call escalate_to_human with the conversation history.
First call get_customer and lookup_order to gather account context, then escalate to a human agent.
rat: O cliente pediu explicitamente um humano — isso é gatilho de escalada — mas uma escalada eficaz exige um handoff com contexto da conta. Coletar get_customer e lookup_order rapidamente e então escalar dá ao humano o que precisa sem impor mais atrito ao cliente. A e B resistem ao pedido explícito e aumentam a frustração; C escala 'às cegas', sem nenhum contexto de conta para o humano agir.

q: A customer support agent calls a payment processing tool to check a refund status. The tool returns status success with empty data array and message 'No refunds found'. The agent's recovery logic treats this as an error and retries. What is wrong with the agent's behaviour?
opts: The agent should retry more times, as the payment system may be slow to process refund records.
The agent incorrectly treats a valid empty result as an error. An empty data array with a success status means the query executed correctly but found no matching records - this requires no retry.
The tool should not return an empty array - it should return an error with isRetryable: false to prevent the retry.
The agent should escalate to a human immediately instead of retrying, as payment queries should never be retried.
rat: Status success + array vazio + 'No refunds found' é resultado válido: a consulta executou e não há registros. Tratar como erro e fazer retry é desperdício — vazio válido ≠ falha. A lógica de recuperação do agente deve diferenciar os dois.

q: A customer support agent handles a multi-issue session. After several turns, the agent refers to 'your recent refund request' instead of the specific $247.83 refund for order #8891. The conversation history is being summarised between turns to manage context length. What is the most effective fix?
opts: Increase the context window size to avoid summarisation entirely
Extract transactional facts (amounts, dates, order numbers) into a persistent case facts block included in every prompt, outside summarised history
Instruct the model to preserve all numerical values during summarisation
Store the full conversation in an external database and retrieve relevant turns on demand
rat: Fatos transacionais (valores, datas, números de pedido) se perdem na sumarização. A correção é extraí-los para um bloco de fatos persistente injetado em todo prompt, fora do histórico sumarizado. Aumentar contexto (A) só adia; instruir a sumarização (C) é probabilístico.

q: A customer support agent has two tools: get_customer_profile (returns name, email, preferences) and get_customer_orders (returns order history with statuses). Users frequently ask 'Tell me about customer X', and the agent calls both tools sequentially, doubling response time. 90% of 'tell me about' queries only need the profile. What is the best approach?
opts: Consolidate both tools into a single get_customer_info tool that returns all customer data in one call.

Update get_customer_profile's description to explicitly state it should be the default for general 'tell me about' queries, and update get_customer_orders to clarify it is only needed when order-specific information is requested.
Add a system prompt instruction: 'When a user says tell me about, only call get_customer_profile.'
Set tool_choice to forced selection of get_customer_profile for all turns to prevent unnecessary order lookups.
rat: 90% das consultas precisam só do perfil: ajustar as descrições para definir o default ('tell me about' → profile) e o critério de uso da segunda tool (só quando pedirem pedidos). tool_choice forçado (D) quebraria os 10% legítimos.

q: A customer support agent integrates with three different backend systems via MCP tools. One system returns dates as Unix timestamps, another returns ISO 8601 strings, and the third returns dates in 'DD/MM/YYYY' format. The agent occasionally misinterprets dates, leading to incorrect order lookup results. What is the correct fix?
opts: Add instructions to the system prompt explaining the three date formats and how to interpret each one
Standardise the backend APIs to all return the same date format
Implement a PreToolUse hook that converts all dates to ISO 8601 before passing them to the tools
Implement a PostToolUse hook that normalises all date formats to ISO 8601 before the model processes the results
rat: Normalização determinística no ponto de entrada dos dados: hook PostToolUse converte todas as datas para ISO 8601 antes de o modelo ver os resultados — elimina a interpretação errada na origem. Instruções no prompt (A) mantêm a chance de erro; padronizar três backends (B) raramente é viável.

q: A customer support agent must redact credit card numbers from its responses before they reach the customer. The current system prompt instructs the agent to replace card numbers with asterisks, but QA testing reveals that 6% of responses still contain unredacted card numbers. What guardrail approach should the architect use?
opts: Rewrite the system prompt with more explicit redaction instructions and add few-shot examples of correct redaction
Implement a PostToolUse hook that applies a regex pattern to detect and redact credit card numbers from all tool results before the model processes them
Implement a PreToolUse hook that blocks any tool call containing a credit card number in its parameters
Remove all tools that might return credit card numbers from the agent's allowedTools
rat: Instrução de prompt deixou passar 6% — inaceitável para PII. Guardrail determinístico: hook PostToolUse aplica regex de cartão sobre os resultados das tools antes de o modelo processá-los; o dado sensível nem entra no contexto, então não pode vazar na resposta.

q: A customer support agent runs to its max_iterations=10 cap on every request, even simple ones. Logs show stop_reason returns 'end_turn' after the third iteration, but the loop ignores it and uses the iteration count as its primary termination signal. What architectural change resolves this?
opts: Increase the iteration cap to 20 so the agent has more room to work on complex requests
Lower the iteration cap (for example to 3) so the loop is forced to terminate earlier on simple requests
Use stop_reason as the primary loop control - exit on 'end_turn', continue on 'tool_use' - and retain the iteration cap only as a safety backstop, not the primary termination mechanism
Parse the agent's response text after each iteration to detect phrases like 'task complete' and terminate early when found
rat: stop_reason deve ser o controle primário do loop ('end_turn' → sai; 'tool_use' → continua); o cap de iterações fica como backstop de segurança apenas. O sintoma (sempre bater no cap) mostra que o sinal correto estava sendo ignorado.

q: A customer support agent searches for a customer named 'Sarah Johnson' and the lookup tool returns three matching accounts with the same name but different addresses and account ages. The agent selects the most recently active account and proceeds with the refund. Later, the customer calls back because the refund was applied to the wrong account. What should the agent have done differently?
opts: Selected the account with the oldest creation date, as long-standing customers are more likely to be the caller.
Asked the customer for additional identifying information - email address, phone number, or order number - to disambiguate the matching accounts.
Processed the refund across all three accounts to ensure the correct customer received it.
Escalated to a human agent immediately because the system returned ambiguous results.
rat: Três contas homônimas = ambiguidade real: a ação correta é pedir dado adicional de identificação (e-mail, telefone, nº do pedido) antes de agir. Heurísticas como 'mais recente' ou 'mais antiga' (A) adivinham — e adivinhar com dinheiro/dados de cliente é inaceitável.

q: A customer support agent uses a token budget of 200k tokens. The system prompt consumes 8k tokens, conversation history takes 120k tokens, and the most recent tool call result returned 65k tokens. The agent is struggling to produce thorough responses. What is the most likely cause and fix?
opts: The model is running out of response tokens - system prompt (8k) + history (120k) + tool result (65k) = 193k, leaving only 7k for the response. Summarise conversation history or truncate verbose tool results to free up response budget
The system prompt is too large at 8k tokens and should be reduced to under 2k to leave more room for the response
The model needs a higher max_tokens parameter to produce longer responses
The conversation history should be cleared entirely to give the model a fresh context for each response
rat: Aritmética de orçamento: 8k + 120k + 65k = 193k de 200k, sobrando ~7k para a resposta — por isso as respostas saem rasas. Sumarizar histórico e/ou truncar tool results verbosos libera orçamento de resposta.

q: A customer support MCP server receives a request to look up a customer record, but the database connection times out after 30 seconds. What is the correct way to structure the MCP error response?
opts: Return an empty result with no error flag, and let the agent infer the failure from the missing data.
Set isError to true and return a structured response with errorCategory: 'transient', isRetryable: true, and a message describing the timeout.
Set isError to true and return errorCategory: 'business', isRetryable: false, since the operation could not be completed.
Throw an unhandled exception to let the MCP framework propagate the error to the agent automatically.
rat: Timeout de conexão é falha transitória clássica: isError: true, errorCategory 'transient', isRetryable: true e mensagem descritiva — o agente sabe que pode tentar de novo. Resultado vazio sem erro (A) mascara a falha; 'business' (C) impediria o retry.

q: A customer support team configures an MCP server for their internal CRM system. The agent frequently ignores the CRM MCP tool and instead uses the built-in Grep tool to search local log files for customer information, producing incomplete results. The MCP tool's description reads: 'CRM tool'. What should the team do first?
opts: Remove the Grep tool from the agent's available tools so it cannot fall back to local file search.
Add a system prompt instruction: 'Always use the CRM tool for customer queries. Never use Grep for customer data.'
Move the MCP server configuration from .mcp.json to ~/.claude.json so it takes higher priority.
Enhance the MCP tool's description to detail its capabilities: full customer records, transaction history, account status, and structured output - explaining what it returns that Grep cannot.
rat: Descrição 'CRM tool' não diz nada — o modelo não tem como saber que a tool retorna registros completos, histórico e status estruturados que o Grep não dá. Melhorar a descrição é o primeiro passo (e o mais barato); remover Grep (A) mutila o agente.

q: A customer writes: "I've been going back and forth on this return for days. I just want to speak to someone who can actually help me." The agent has confirmed via lookup_order that the return is straightforward—within policy and eligible for immediate processing. What should the agent do?
opts: Acknowledge frustration, inform them this is resolvable now, and offer to complete it or escalate
Call `escalate_to_human` immediately to honor the customer's request
Process the refund via `process_refund` to resolve the underlying issue, then inform them it's complete
Ask what specifically hasn't worked in previous attempts before deciding whether to escalate or resolve automatically
rat: O caso já foi confirmado como elegível e dentro da política: reconhecer a frustração, informar que é resolvível agora e oferecer concluir ou escalar maximiza resolução no primeiro contato respeitando a escolha do cliente. Escalar direto (B) desperdiça uma resolução imediata; processar o reembolso sem consentimento (C) age sem confirmação do cliente; pedir mais detalhes (D) só adiciona atrito a um caso já resolvido.

q: A customer-support agent runs an agentic loop. Each turn it inspects stop_reason; when the value is tool_use it executes the requested tool and receives the tool's output. Before it calls the model again, what must the agent do with that output?
opts: Append the tool result to the conversation history as a new message, then send the full updated conversation back to the model on the next call
Replace the previous assistant message with the tool output to keep the context window small
Send only the tool output to the model on the next call, since the model already produced the tool request
Store the tool output in an external store and pass a reference id to the model on the next call
rat: No loop agêntico, o resultado da tool deve ser anexado ao histórico como nova mensagem (tool_result) e a conversa completa reenviada — o modelo é stateless entre chamadas. Substituir (B) ou enviar só o resultado (C) quebra o contexto do loop.

q: A data extraction pipeline extracts financial figures from annual reports. The pipeline cites sources using inline text references like 'According to page 12 of the 2024 Annual Report, revenue was $4.2B.' When the extracted data is consumed by a downstream analytics system, the source attribution is consistently lost. What is the root cause and fix?
opts: The downstream system strips text formatting. Fix by making the citations bold or using a special delimiter

Inline prose citations are fragile because downstream systems rewrite, parse, or restructure text. Fix by outputting structured claim-source mappings where each extracted value is paired with its source document, page, and date as structured data fields
The downstream system needs to be modified to preserve all input text verbatim without any transformation
Add a separate attribution database that the downstream system can query to look up sources for any extracted value
rat: Citação em prosa ('segundo a página 12...') se perde quando o texto é parseado/reescrito pelo sistema downstream. A correção é proveniência estruturada: cada valor extraído acompanhado de campos de dados (documento, página, data) que sobrevivem ao pipeline.

q: A developer asks Claude Code to explain the order processing pipeline before refactoring it. The pipeline spans 15 classes across 4 packages with deep inheritance and callback chains. Claude Code reads 3 files and produces a superficial summary that misses the callback chain entirely. What approach would produce a more thorough analysis?
opts: Re-prompt Claude Code with more specific instructions listing all 15 classes to read

Use an Explore subagent configured for verbose discovery, which will systematically trace the pipeline across classes, follow inheritance hierarchies, and map callback chains before producing a comprehensive analysis
Switch to plan mode and ask Claude Code to create a plan for understanding the pipeline
Run multiple Claude Code sessions, each analysing a different package, then manually combine the results
rat: Análise profunda de fluxo multi-classe é caso para subagente Explore configurado para descoberta verbosa: rastreia o pipeline sistematicamente (herança, callbacks) isolando o volume de leitura do contexto principal, e devolve a análise consolidada.

q: A developer asks the agent to investigate why a specific API endpoint intermittently returns 500 errors. The codebase has 200+ files and the developer doesn't know which components are involved. The agent must trace the error through routing, middleware, business logic, and database layers. What task decomposition approach would be most effective?
opts: Have the agent first create a comprehensive plan mapping all code paths through the endpoint before beginning any file exploration or code reading.
Have the agent dynamically generate investigation subtasks based on what it discovers at each step, adapting its exploration plan as new information about the error path emerges.

Define a fixed sequence of investigation steps upfront—grep for error patterns, then read error handlers, then check database queries, then examine middleware—executing each step regardless of intermediate findings.
Run parallel worker agents that simultaneously investigate all four layers, then
rat: Depurar erro intermitente em 200+ arquivos exige decomposição dinâmica: cada descoberta redefine onde investigar em seguida. Planejar todos os caminhos antes de ler qualquer arquivo (A) é impraticável; sequência fixa ignorando achados intermediários (C) desperdiça esforço; paralelizar as quatro camadas de cara (D) dispersa recursos antes de saber onde está o problema.

q: A developer extracting the notification subsystem from a monolith faces 12 cross-module dependencies, 3 messaging patterns, and several valid extraction strategies. They start in direct execution mode. After moving 8 files, the chosen approach breaks circular dependencies with the user-profile module. What should they have done differently?
opts: Used direct execution but with more detailed upfront instructions specifying how to handle each dependency
Used plan mode to explore the codebase, map all 12 module dependencies, identify circular dependencies, and evaluate extraction strategies before committing to an approach
Used direct execution but processed only 2 files at a time to catch problems earlier
Delegated the entire extraction to a subagent to isolate the risk
rat: Alta complexidade + dependências circulares + múltiplas estratégias válidas é o cenário clássico de plan mode: explorar e mapear dependências antes de executar. Execução direta descobriu o problema no meio do caminho, com 8 arquivos já movidos.

q: A developer has been using the data platform agent in a single extended session for two hours, running complex federated queries, debugging SQL syntax, and iterating on report formatting. The agent now produces noticeably worse SQL and occasionally references table schemas from earlier queries that have since been corrected. What is the most effective intervention?
opts: Use the /compact command, optionally passing focus instructions, to summarise the conversation so far and free context budget for current-state reasoning.
Start a completely new session and re-establish all context from scratch.
Increase the model's temperature parameter to improve creativity and reduce fixation on stale patterns.
Add a system prompt instruction telling the agent to ignore all schema information from more than 30 minutes ago.
rat: /compact resume a conversa preservando o essencial (aceita instruções de foco) e libera orçamento de contexto — resolve a degradação e as referências a schemas obsoletos sem perder o trabalho da sessão. Sessão nova (B) joga fora duas horas de contexto útil.

q: A developer is exploring two different strategies for decomposing the monolith's order processing module: (1) splitting by business capability (orders, payments, shipping) or (2) splitting by data ownership (order-db, payment-db, shipping-db). They want to explore both approaches in parallel without losing either analysis. What is the correct Claude Code approach?
opts: Open two terminal tabs and run separate Claude Code sessions with different instructions in each
Use fork_session to create two parallel exploration branches from the current session's baseline, one for each decomposition strategy
Use --resume to alternate between the two strategies in a single session, relying on conversation history to separate the analyses
Instruct the agent to evaluate both strategies sequentially in the same session, then compare results
rat: fork_session cria ramificações paralelas a partir do mesmo estado-base da sessão, preservando o contexto comum e mantendo as duas análises separadas. Duas sessões novas (A) perdem o baseline; alternar com --resume (C) mistura os contextos.

q: A DevOps team's polyglot codebase has Terraform files in terraform/, Kubernetes manifests in k8s/, and Dockerfiles scattered throughout various service directories. They want Claude Code to automatically apply infrastructure-specific conventions when editing each type of file, without loading all conventions for every session. What is the correct approach?
opts: Create three directory-level CLAUDE.md files: one in terraform/, one in k8s/, and one in the project root for Docker conventions
Add all infrastructure conventions to the root CLAUDE.md with clear section headings so the model knows which section applies
Create a single .claude/rules/infrastructure.md file with paths: ["**/*"] containing all infrastructure conventions
Create three .claude/rules/ files: terraform.md with paths for terraform files, kubernetes.md with paths for k8s files, and docker.md with paths for Dockerfile patterns
rat: Dockerfiles estão espalhados por vários diretórios — CLAUDE.md por diretório (A) não cobre esse caso. .claude/rules/ com frontmatter paths por padrão de arquivo (terraform/**, k8s/**, **/Dockerfile*) carrega cada convenção só quando o arquivo correspondente é editado.

q: A docs team has Claude Code synthesise an API reference guide from source across 50 modules. The guide states 'the authentication service supports OAuth2, SAML, and API key authentication' with no indication of which source files informed this. Six months later, after a major refactor, the team needs to verify whether the statement is still accurate. What is the core provenance failure and how should it be prevented?
opts: The failure is that the guide was not version-controlled. Storing it in git would allow the team to trace the statement back to the commit that generated it

The failure is loss of claim-to-source traceability during synthesis. Each factual claim should carry structured provenance linking it to specific source files and retrieval timestamps, enabling targeted re-verification of affected claims after refactoring
The failure is that Claude Code hallucinated the authentication methods. The team should run a separate verification pass to confirm each claim against the source code
The failure is that the documentation was generated in a single pass. Processing modules incrementally with human review after each module would catch inaccuracies before synthesis
rat: A falha é perda de rastreabilidade claim→fonte na síntese. Cada afirmação factual deve carregar proveniência estruturada (arquivos-fonte, timestamp), permitindo re-verificação direcionada após refactors: mudou o arquivo X, revalide as claims ligadas a X. Git (A) versiona o texto, não a origem das claims.

q: A docs team processes a 500,000-line codebase by having Claude Code read files and generate docs, instructing it to 'summarise each module after documenting it, then discard the detailed source from context.' After processing 20 modules, the docs for module 21 reference class names from module 3 that were renamed in module 12. What caused this failure?
opts: Progressive summarisation lost the rename detail: when module 12's summary replaced its source code, the rename was condensed to 'various refactoring changes', so the agent retained module 3's original class names from its earlier summary
The agent's context window overflowed, causing it to hallucinate class names from earlier modules
Claude Code cached module 3's source code and served the stale version instead of the updated file on disk
The model confused similar class names because it processed too many modules in a single session
rat: Sumarização progressiva com perda: quando o módulo 12 foi resumido, o rename virou 'various refactoring changes' e o detalhe se perdeu; o agente seguiu usando os nomes antigos vindos do resumo do módulo 3. Resumos devem preservar fatos críticos (renames, breaking changes).

q: A documentation generation pipeline uses Claude Code to produce docs from three sources: source code comments, existing wiki pages, and API schema files. During a run, the wiki page retrieval fails with a timeout, but the source code and API schema are available. The pipeline currently halts entirely on any source failure. What is the best error handling strategy?
opts: Retry the wiki retrieval three times with exponential backoff. If all retries fail, halt the pipeline to prevent incomplete documentation.

Return structured error context for the wiki failure (failure type, source URL, partial results if any), proceed with available sources, and mark the generated documentation with explicit gaps noting which sections lack wiki-sourced content.
Silently skip the wiki source and generate documentation from the remaining two sources without noting the omission.
Use the source code comments to infer what the wiki pages would have contained, filling in the gaps with generated content.
rat: Degradação graciosa: registrar erro estruturado da fonte que falhou, prosseguir com as fontes disponíveis e marcar explicitamente no documento as lacunas. Parar tudo (A) desperdiça o que funciona; omitir silenciosamente (C) engana; inventar conteúdo (D) fabrica documentação.

q: A documentation team must update architecture guides across 25 microservice directories after a platform migration. Working in direct execution mode, a team member updates three services before noticing the results are inconsistent: some guides still mention the old platform, others use inconsistent terminology. What went wrong and what is the correct approach?
opts: The team member should have used plan mode to establish a consistent update strategy, terminology glossary, and section-by-section template before executing changes across all 25 services
Direct execution was correct but should have been done in a single continuous session to maintain consistency through conversation context
The team member should have created a custom slash command that hardcodes the exact text replacements for each service
The team member should have used the Explore subagent to discover all affected files first, then made all changes in a single batch
rat: Trabalho repetitivo em 25 diretórios exige padronização prévia: plan mode para definir estratégia, glossário de terminologia e template por seção antes de executar — a inconsistência veio de executar sem padrão estabelecido.

q: A documentation team needs to simultaneously update API reference docs for three independent microservices after a breaking change. Each update requires reading source code, updating Markdown files, and validating links. A single Claude Code session would exhaust the context window trying to hold all three services' code simultaneously. What is the recommended approach?
opts: Process the three services sequentially in the same session, running /compact between each service to free context
Use git worktree to create three parallel branches, each with its own Claude Code session working on one service's documentation, then merge the results
Create a single skill with context: fork that processes all three services in parallel within one session
Split the documentation files into smaller chunks and process each chunk in a separate API call using the batch API
rat: Três serviços independentes que não cabem num contexto: git worktree dá a cada sessão de Claude Code seu diretório e branch isolados, com contexto dedicado a um serviço; merge ao final. /compact sequencial (A) mantém o gargalo de contexto e é mais lento.

q: A documentation team needs to update all references to a renamed API endpoint across their 500,000-line codebase. They know the old endpoint name (POST /api/v1/users/create) but not which files reference it. After locating the files, they need to replace the old endpoint with the new one (POST /api/v2/users). What is the correct tool sequence?
opts: Use Glob for '**/*.md' to find all documentation files, then Read each one to check for the old endpoint, then use Edit to update matches.
Use Read on the API router file to find all endpoint definitions, then manually trace each reference.
Use Grep for '/api/v1/users/create' to find all files containing the old endpoint, then use Edit to replace the old endpoint with the new one in each matched file.
Use Bash with sed to perform a global find-and-replace across all files in a single command.
rat: Você conhece a string exata e não sabe onde ela aparece: Grep localiza todos os arquivos com o endpoint antigo, Edit aplica a substituição revisável em cada um. Glob por *.md (A) perde arquivos de outros tipos; sed global (D) é cego e arriscado num repo de 500k linhas.

q: A documentation team wants to archive the full conversation transcript to a log file every time Claude Code runs /compact, so that context lost during compaction can be reviewed later. Which hook configuration achieves this?
opts: A PostToolUse hook on Write that snapshots the transcript whenever a file is written, on the assumption /compact will eventually run
A PreCompact hook that writes the current transcript to a timestamped log file before /compact summarises the conversation
A PreToolUse hook configured on a built-in 'Compact' tool, matching tool name 'Compact'
A PostToolUse hook on all tools that appends each tool result to a running log file, creating a continuous archive
rat: PreCompact é o hook disparado antes da compactação — o ponto exato para salvar o transcript completo em log antes que o conteúdo seja resumido. Nenhum hook de Write ou PostToolUse (A/D) captura o momento do /compact.

q: A documentation team working across a 500,000-line codebase notices that Claude Code's responses become less specific after extensive file exploration. What is the term for this degradation and what is the primary mitigation in Claude Code?
opts: Token exhaustion - mitigated by increasing the model's max_tokens parameter
Context degradation - mitigated by using scratchpad files to persist key findings outside the conversation context, and delegating exploration to subagents to isolate verbose output
Hallucination drift - mitigated by lowering the temperature to zero for deterministic output
Prompt injection - mitigated by sanitising file contents before they enter the context window
rat: O termo é context degradation (degradação de contexto após exploração extensa). Mitigações canônicas no Claude Code: persistir descobertas-chave em arquivos scratchpad fora do contexto e delegar exploração verbosa a subagentes, isolando o ruído.

q: A legal document extraction pipeline has been running for three months with calibrated confidence thresholds. Extractions above 90% confidence are auto-approved without human review, saving 60% of reviewer time. A new client submits contracts written in an unusual two-column legal format the system has not encountered before. The system reports 92% confidence on these extractions. What is the appropriate safeguard?
opts: Maintain stratified random sampling of high-confidence extractions across document types, which would detect the elevated error rate on the new format before significant damage occurs
Trust the calibrated confidence threshold since it was validated against labelled data and the system reports 92% confidence, which exceeds the 90% auto-approval threshold
Raise the confidence threshold from 90% to 98% for all document types to account for novel formats
Add a document format classifier that flags unknown formats for mandatory human review before any auto-approval
rat: Calibração de confiança só vale para a distribuição em que foi validada — formato novo torna o 92% não confiável (é exatamente o caso de alta confiança e alto erro). Amostragem estratificada contínua das extrações auto-aprovadas, por tipo de documento, é a salvaguarda que detecta a taxa de erro elevada antes de dano significativo.

q: A multi-agent research system finds conflicting data on a pharmaceutical compound's efficacy. A 2022 peer-reviewed clinical trial reports 78% efficacy, while a 2024 preprint reports 45% efficacy with a larger sample size. The synthesis agent must produce a recommendation for a medical advisory board. Which approach correctly handles the conflicting sources?
opts: Use the 2024 preprint as it has a larger sample size and is more recent, noting it supersedes the 2022 study
Average the two values (61.5%) and present it as the best estimate with a note about variance between studies

Present both findings with full provenance: source identity, publication date, peer-review status, sample size, methodology, and any methodological differences that may explain the discrepancy. Let the advisory board weigh the evidence
Exclude both findings and report that no reliable consensus exists, recommending further research before any conclusions
rat: Em conflito de fontes para decisão de alto risco, o sistema não deve arbitrar: apresenta ambos os achados com proveniência completa (data, peer-review, amostra, metodologia) e deixa os especialistas humanos pesarem. Escolher um (A), fazer média (B) ou descartar tudo (D) destrói informação.

q: A multi-agent research system has a coordinator that spawns a web search subagent and a document analysis subagent sequentially across separate API turns. The web search completes in 8 seconds and the document analysis completes in 12 seconds, giving a total latency of 20 seconds. The two subagents are investigating independent topics and do not depend on each other's results. How should the architect reduce this latency?
opts: Merge the web search and document analysis into a single subagent to reduce coordination overhead
Have the coordinator emit both Task tool calls in a single response to spawn both subagents in parallel, reducing total latency to roughly 12 seconds
Allow the web search subagent to directly invoke the document analysis subagent after it finishes, removing the coordinator round-trip
Use fork_session to split the coordinator into two parallel branches, one for each subagent
rat: Tarefas independentes devem ser paralelizadas: o coordenador emite as duas chamadas Task no mesmo turno da resposta, e os subagentes rodam em paralelo. A latência total passa a ser a do mais lento (~12s) em vez da soma (20s).

q: A platform has search_knowledge_base and process_action tools. The agent picks search_knowledge_base for cancel my subscription requests. After adding subscription cancellations to process_action's description, it now picks process_action for knowledge queries. What is the most effective solution?
opts: Add boundary descriptions to both tools: process_action should state use when the customer wants to execute a cancellation and search_knowledge_base should state use when the customer wants to learn about the cancellation process without taking action.
Remove the word cancellation from both tool descriptions to eliminate the keyword conflict entirely.
Consolidate search_knowledge_base and process_action into a single tool that determines the correct action based on user intent.
Add few-shot examples to the system prompt showing five cancellation scenarios with the correct tool for each.
rat: Descrições de fronteira explícitas resolvem a ambiguidade nos dois sentidos: process_action = 'quando o cliente quer executar o cancelamento'; search_knowledge_base = 'quando quer aprender sobre o processo sem agir'. Remover a palavra (B) tira informação; consolidar (C) esconde o problema.

q: A PR modifying 14 files receives inconsistent review: detailed feedback on some files, superficial comments on others, and contradictory findings. What is the best restructuring?
opts: Switch to a higher-tier model with a larger context window
Run three independent passes on the full PR and only flag issues found in at least two passes
Split into per-file local analysis passes plus a separate cross-file integration pass
Require developers to split large PRs into smaller submissions of 3-4 files
rat: Mesmo padrão da questão 54: análise local por arquivo (atenção dedicada e consistente) + passe separado de integração para questões entre arquivos. Votação em múltiplos passes (B) triplica custo sem resolver a diluição.

q: A query_postgres MCP tool returns 500 on transient server overload and 404 on missing tables. The agent retries both identically, exhausting retries on missing tables and then failing to retry once the server recovers. What change to the error response structure would fix this?
opts: Return all errors with the MCP isError flag set to true and include the HTTP status code in the error message text so the agent can parse it.

Classify errors into structured categories — transient (server overload, network timeout) and validation (missing table, invalid column) — with the MCP isError flag and a category field, so the agent retries only transient errors.
Remove the retry logic entirely and escalate all errors to the user immediately for manual resolution.
Increase the retry limit from 3 to 10 so the agent has enough attempts to cover both transient and permanent errors.
rat: O agente precisa distinguir classes de erro para decidir o retry: transient (sobrecarga, timeout) → retry com backoff; validation (tabela inexistente) → não adianta repetir. isError + campo de categoria estruturado dá essa informação. Parsear mensagem de texto (A) é frágil.

q: A refactoring agent decomposes a legacy Java monolith into microservices. Its loop terminates by parsing the assistant's last message for the phrase 'refactoring complete', running 20 iterations even on simple classes. Logs show stop_reason returns 'end_turn' after iteration 4. What is the correct fix?
opts: Improve the phrase detection to also check for 'finished', 'done', and 'all changes applied'
Replace the natural language parsing with stop_reason inspection - exit the loop when stop_reason is 'end_turn' and continue only when it is 'tool_use'
Lower the iteration cap to 5 so the agent stops sooner for simple classes
Add a tool that the agent must call explicitly to signal completion, and check for that tool call each iteration
rat: stop_reason é o sinal correto de término do loop: 'end_turn' = modelo concluiu; 'tool_use' = continue executando. Parsear frases em linguagem natural é frágil (o modelo disse 'end_turn' na iteração 4 e o loop rodou 20). Melhorar a lista de frases (A) mantém a fragilidade.

q: A research agent calls an external API via an MCP server. After the 30th query in a batch of 50, the API starts returning HTTP 429 errors. The MCP server returns a generic 'Request failed' for every failure, so the agent abandons the batch after three consecutive failures. What MCP server change would most improve resilience?
opts: Implement automatic retry with exponential backoff inside the MCP server, hiding rate limits from the agent entirely.
Return structured errors with errorCategory: 'transient', isRetryable: true, and include a retryAfterMs field indicating how long to wait before the next attempt.
Return errorCategory: 'business', isRetryable: false to tell the agent to stop making requests entirely.
Queue all 50 requests at the MCP server level and process them sequentially with built-in rate limiting.
rat: HTTP 429 é rate limit: erro transitório e retryable, e o campo retryAfterMs diz quanto esperar — o agente pausa e completa o batch em vez de abandoná-lo. Esconder tudo no server (A) tira do agente a chance de replanejar; 'business/not retryable' (C) mataria o batch.

q: A search_papers MCP tool has three failure patterns: (1) the upstream academic API returns HTTP 503 during peak hours, (2) users request papers from a restricted journal the system has no licence for, and (3) the agent submits a malformed DOI like 'doi-1234' that fails the input regex. The team wants structured error metadata so agents can handle each case differently. Which errorCategory and isRetryable combination is correct for all three?
opts: All three should be errorCategory: 'transient', isRetryable: true, since they all prevent the tool from completing its task.
HTTP 503: transient/retryable; restricted journal: business/not retryable; malformed DOI: validation/retryable.
HTTP 503: transient/retryable; restricted journal: transient/retryable; malformed DOI: transient/retryable.
HTTP 503: validation/retryable; restricted journal: business/not retryable; malformed DOI: business/not retryable.
rat: Erros devem ser categorizados: 503 é transitório (retry resolve); falta de licença é regra de negócio (retry nunca resolve, isRetryable: false); DOI malformado é erro de validação — retryable porque o agente pode corrigir o input e tentar de novo. Tratar tudo como 'transient' (A/C) causa retries inúteis.

q: A support agent has process_refund and adjust_billing tools with clearly differentiated descriptions, yet still occasionally calls adjust_billing for refund requests. The system prompt contains the instruction: 'For any billing-related concerns, prioritise resolving them quickly using available billing tools.' What is the root cause?
opts: The tool descriptions are still too similar and need further differentiation with explicit boundary statements.

The keyword-sensitive instruction in the system prompt creates an unintended association between refund requests (which mention billing) and the adjust_billing tool, overriding the improved descriptions.
The model needs few-shot examples demonstrating that refund requests should use process_refund, not adjust_billing.
The two tools should be consolidated into a single financial_operation tool that handles both refunds and billing adjustments.
rat: A instrução 'resolva rápido usando as ferramentas de billing' cria associação por palavra-chave: pedido de refund menciona billing → adjust_billing. A instrução keyword-sensível no prompt está sabotando as descrições corretas das tools.

q: A support agent tries to refund $850, but policy caps automated refunds at $500. The MCP tool returns 'Operation failed' as its error. The agent retries three times before escalating to a human. What change to the tool's error response would most effectively prevent this behaviour?
opts: Include a longer error message explaining the policy: 'Operation failed because the refund amount of $850 exceeds the $500 automated refund limit'.
Set the MCP isError flag and add a retry-after header so the agent waits before retrying.
Return a structured error with errorCategory: 'business', isRetryable: false, and a customer-friendly description explaining the policy limit.
Catch the error in the agent's system prompt with an instruction: 'Never retry refund operations that fail'.
rat: Limite de política é erro de negócio: errorCategory 'business', isRetryable: false — o agente para de tentar imediatamente e pode explicar o motivo ao cliente. Mensagem mais longa em texto livre (A) não é sinal estruturado; retry-after (B) sugere que repetir resolveria.

q: A support system processes two request types: password resets (high volume, latency-sensitive) and account migrations (low volume, complex, can complete overnight). Both share one synchronous API endpoint at identical priority. At peak hours, migrations consume rate limit capacity and password-reset latency exceeds thresholds. What is the best approach?
opts: Pause all account migration requests during business hours and process them only between midnight and 6am
Route password resets through the real-time API for low-latency responses, and route account migrations through the batch processing API for high-throughput, latency-tolerant processing
Implement a priority queue where password resets always execute before migration requests, using a single API endpoint
Add more API keys to increase total rate limit capacity so both workloads can run simultaneously without contention
rat: Roteamento por perfil de carga: password reset (sensível a latência) → API em tempo real; migrações (toleram horas) → Batch API, que tem throughput alto e não compete pelo rate limit do tráfego interativo.

q: A team runs a coordinator agent that delegates refactoring work to three specialist subagents: a schema-migration agent, an API-layer agent, and a test-update agent. The test-update agent frequently produces tests that reference database schemas the schema-migration agent has already renamed. The subagents do not communicate with each other. What is the root cause?
opts: The subagents need a shared message bus so the test-update agent can query the schema-migration agent for the latest schema names
The test-update agent should have read access to the schema migration files so it can discover the new names itself
The coordinator is not passing the schema-migration agent's output (including renamed schemas) as context when delegating to the test-update agent
The schema-migration agent and test-update agent should run sequentially rather than in parallel to avoid race conditions
rat: Em arquiteturas coordenador/subagente, cada subagente tem contexto isolado. É responsabilidade do coordenador propagar os resultados de um subagente (os schemas renomeados) como contexto ao delegar a tarefa dependente. Message bus e acesso a arquivos contornam o problema; sequenciar sem passar contexto não resolve.

q: A team wants their CI pipeline to run Claude Code for both PR code review and automated test generation. The PR review should output structured JSON for integration with their review dashboard, while the test generation should produce standard text output. How should the two pipeline steps be configured?
opts: Run both steps with -p and configure the review dashboard to parse plain text output from both steps
Run the review step with -p --output-format json and the test generation step with -p only, as separate non-interactive invocations
Run both steps in a single Claude Code session with -p, using different prompts to request different output formats
Run both steps with --output-format json and have the test generation step extract code from the JSON response
rat: Cada passo é uma invocação não interativa separada (-p): o review com --output-format json para o dashboard parsear; a geração de testes com -p simples, saída em texto. Formatos de saída são por invocação, não por prompt dentro de uma sessão (C).

q: A tool returns an empty array after a customer lookup. The agent retries 3 times, then escalates to a human agent. Analysis shows the customer's account simply does not exist. What is the root cause of this wasted effort?
opts: The retry limit is too low - increasing to 5 retries would resolve the issue.
The tool does not distinguish between access failures and valid empty results, so the agent treats 'no matches' as a retriable failure.
The escalation threshold is too aggressive - the agent should exhaust more retries before involving a human.
The system prompt should instruct the agent not to retry customer lookups.
rat: Mesmo padrão de falha de design: a tool não separa 'não encontrei' (resultado vazio válido — não faz sentido retry) de 'não consegui acessar'. O agente gastou retries e escalação humana num caso de 'conta não existe'.

q: A user is expanding the research system beyond its single web search agent by adding specialized data sources. They add a financial API agent, a news monitoring agent, and a patent analysis agent. The synthesis agent combines these into executive briefings. Currently, it converts everything to bullet points, causing financial comparisons to lose tabular clarity and news summaries to lose narrative flow. What change would most improve briefing quality?
opts: Standardize all subagent outputs to prose summaries with inline citations.
Add a format conversion layer between subagents and synthesis that transforms all outputs to a common intermediate representation.
Update the synthesis agent to render each content type appropriately—financial data as tables, news as prose.
Standardize all subagent outputs to JSON with fields for claim, evidence, source, and confidence.
rat: O problema é o agente de síntese achatar tudo em bullet points; a correção é ele renderizar cada tipo de conteúdo no formato adequado — dados financeiros como tabelas, notícias como prosa. Padronizar tudo em prosa (A) ou em JSON (D) repete o erro de formato único, e uma camada de conversão intermediária (B) adiciona complexidade sem resolver a renderização final.

q: A web search agent has 9 tools: web_search, url_fetch, html_parse, pdf_extract, image_ocr, translate, summarise, keyword_extract, sentiment_analysis. In testing it frequently calls summarise and sentiment_analysis when it should only fetch raw data. How should the architect fix this?
opts: Add system prompt instructions telling the web search agent to only use data fetching tools and ignore analysis tools

Reduce the web search agent's allowedTools to only the 4-5 tools relevant to data fetching (web_search, url_fetch, html_parse, pdf_extract) and move analysis tools to the appropriate specialist agents
Keep all 9 tools but implement PreToolUse hooks that block the web search agent from calling summarise and sentiment_analysis
Merge the web search and synthesis agents into a single agent since they share some tools
rat: O princípio é escopo mínimo de ferramentas por agente (4–7 tools relevantes). Reduzir allowedTools é determinístico; instruções de prompt (A) são probabilísticas e hooks (C) bloqueiam mas não impedem a tentativa. O agente não deve ter ferramentas fora do seu papel.

q: After adding an MCP server with specialized code refactoring tools (`extract_function`, `rename_variable`, `inline_function`), you notice the agent still uses basic text manipulation via Write and Bash sed commands for refactoring tasks. The MCP server is connected and healthy. Examining the configuration, you find each MCP tool has a minimal description like "`extract_function`: extracts a function from code." What's the most effective way to improve adoption of the MCP refactoring tools?
opts: Implement a request classifier that detects refactoring intent and automatically routes those requests to the MCP server before the agent processes them.
Remove the Write tool from the agent's configuration for refactoring sessions so it must use the MCP tools for code modifications.
Accept this as expected behavior since simpler tools like sed are more predictable than specialized refactoring tools.
Enhance the MCP tool descriptions to explain when each tool is preferable to text manipulation and clarify expected inputs and outputs.
rat: A seleção de ferramentas é guiada pelas descrições; enriquecer as descrições dos tools MCP explicando quando são preferíveis à manipulação de texto e clarificando entradas/saídas resolve a causa raiz da baixa adoção. Um classificador de intenção (A) adiciona complexidade externa, remover Write (B) quebra outros usos legítimos, e aceitar sed (C) mantém refatorações frágeis.

q: After deployment, you find that 12% of extractions contain semantic errors that pass JSON schema validation (e.g., a duration like "30 minutes" incorrectly placed in an ingredient quantity field). Human reviewers have capacity to check only 20% of extractions. Which approach most effectively allocates reviewer attention?
opts: Have the model output field-level confidence scores, then calibrate review thresholds using a labeled validation set.
Randomly sample 20% of extractions for review, using corrections to track accuracy and identify error patterns.
Prioritize review of all extractions where required fields are empty or explicitly marked as not found.
Review all extractions from documents with formatting anomalies such as unusual layouts or mixed content types.
rat: Confiança em nível de campo calibrada com conjunto de validação rotulado direciona os 20% de capacidade de revisão exatamente para as extrações mais prováveis de conter erro semântico. Amostragem aleatória (B) desperdiça revisores em extrações corretas; campos vazios (C) não capturam erros semânticos com valores presentes-porém-errados; anomalias de formatação (D) é proxy fraco para erro semântico.

q: After implementing tool use with strict schema definitions, JSON syntax errors are eliminated, but 5% of extractions still have valid JSON with empty arrays or null values for required fields like citations and methodology. Spot-checking reveals that source documents contain this information, but in varied formats—inline citations vs. bibliographies, methodology sections vs. details embedded in introductions. What's the most effective way to address these failures?
opts: Implement retry logic that re-sends requests when validation detects empty required fields.
Build a regex-based post-processing layer that scans source documents for citation patterns and methodology keywords, populating empty fields when the model fails to extract.
Modify your schema to make citations and methodology optional, and flag incomplete records for manual review rather than failing validation.
Add few-shot examples demonstrating extractions from documents with varied structures–showing how to identify citations in different formats and locate methodology details across section types.
rat: O problema é capacidade de reconhecimento em formatos variados — few-shot mostrando citações e metodologia em posições/formatos diferentes ensina exatamente isso. Retry (A) repete a mesma falha; regex (B) é frágil demais para variação semântica; tornar campos opcionais (C) apenas esconde a lacuna em vez de corrigi-la.

q: After integrating a local MCP server providing code analysis tools (`analyze_dependencies`, `find_dead_code`, `calculate_complexity`), you verify the server is healthy and tools appear in the tools/list response. However, you observe that the agent consistently uses Grep to search for import statements instead of calling `analyze_dependencies`—even when users explicitly ask about "code dependencies." Examining tool definitions reveals: MCP: `analyze_dependencies` - "Analyzes dependency graph" Built-in: Grep - "Search file contents for a pattern using regular expressions. Returns matching lines with line numbers and surrounding context." What's the most effective approach to improve the agent's selection of MCP tools?
opts: Remove Grep from available tools when the MCP server is connected to eliminate functional overlap.
Add routing instructions to the system prompt specifying that dependency-related questions should use MCP tools rather than Grep.
Split `analyze_dependencies` into granular tools (`list_imports`, `resolve_transitive_deps`, `detect_circular_deps`) so each has a focused purpose less likely to overlap with Grep.
Expand MCP tool descriptions to detail capabilities and outputs—e.g., "Builds dependency graph showing direct imports, transitive dependencies, and cycles."
rat: Descrições ricas explicando que analyze_dependencies constrói grafo com imports diretos, dependências transitivas e ciclos dão ao modelo o critério para preferi-lo ao Grep — a seleção de tools é guiada primariamente pelas descrições. Remover Grep (A) mutila capacidades legítimas, regras no prompt (B) são menos robustas que boas descrições, e fragmentar o tool (C) não resolve a descrição pobre.

q: After investigating a billing dispute over 25+ turns, you've identified that duplicate charges occurred due to a payment gateway timeout triggering retry logic. The required refund ($847) exceeds your $500 authorization limit. You need to call escalate_to_human, and the human agent won't have access to your conversation transcript. What context should you pass to enable effective resolution?
opts: The customer's original complaint verbatim plus the tool result excerpts showing duplicate transactions.
A structured summary: customer ID, root cause, refund amount, and recommended action.
The complete conversation transcript with all tool results.
Your diagnosis and the refund amount only.
rat: Sem acesso ao transcript, o humano precisa de um resumo estruturado e acionável: ID do cliente, causa raiz, valor e ação recomendada. O verbatim + excertos (A) obriga o humano a rediagnosticar; o transcript completo (C) não está disponível no cenário e seria pesado; só diagnóstico e valor (D) omite identificação do cliente e ação recomendada.

q: After the web search agent and document analysis agent complete their tasks, the coordinator invokes the synthesis agent. However, the synthesis agent responds that it cannot complete the task because no research findings were provided. What is the most likely cause of this issue?
opts: The synthesis agent's context window is not large enough to hold the combined outputs from both previous agents.
The coordinator did not include the outputs from the previous agents in the synthesis agent's prompt.
The subagents need to share a single API connection to enable automatic context sharing between invocations.
The synthesis agent needs tools that can fetch results directly from the other agents' conversation histories.
rat: Subagentes têm contextos isolados: os resultados só chegam à síntese se o coordenador os incluir explicitamente no prompt de invocação. Limite de contexto (A) causaria truncamento, não ausência total; não existe compartilhamento automático via conexão de API (C); subagentes não buscam nos históricos uns dos outros (D).

q: After the web search agent finds 25 sources (120K tokens of raw content), the document analysis agent extracts key insights (15K tokens), and the synthesis agent produces a coherent narrative draft (3K tokens), the coordinator must pass context to the report generation agent for the final output with proper source citations. What context-passing strategy provides the best balance of completeness and efficiency?
opts: Pass only the synthesis draft and have a separate post-processing pipeline match claims to sources and insert citations after the report is generated.
Pass the synthesis draft along with a structured source index that maps key claims to their source URLs and relevant excerpts.
Pass a condensed summary of all prior stages that preserves the main findings and attributes them to sources by name only.
Pass the full accumulated context from all prior agents.
rat: Passar o rascunho de síntese junto com um índice estruturado que mapeia afirmações-chave a URLs e trechos das fontes dá ao gerador de relatório tudo que precisa para citar corretamente, sem os 120K tokens brutos. Pós-processar citações depois (A) é frágil, atribuir por nome apenas (C) perde a rastreabilidade dos trechos, e passar todo o contexto acumulado (D) estoura eficiência e possivelmente o contexto.

q: After your daily batch of 10,000 documents completes, 300 documents (3%) failed with "context_length_exceeded" errors. The results file identifies each failure by custom_id. What's the most cost-effective approach to process these failures?
opts: Reprocess the entire batch with prompt caching enabled to reduce the cost of retrying requests with identical system prompts
Resubmit only the 300 failed documents after chunking them into smaller pieces, then combine the partial extractions
Resubmit the entire 10,000 document batch using a model tier with a larger context window
Increase the max_tokens parameter for the 300 failed documents and resubmit them in a new batch
rat: Como as falhas foram por context_length_exceeded e cada falha é identificável por custom_id, o custo-efetivo é reenviar apenas os 300 documentos falhos após dividi-los em pedaços menores que caibam no contexto. Reprocessar todo o lote (A e C) paga novamente por 9.700 documentos que já tiveram sucesso, e aumentar max_tokens (D) não resolve — o erro é de contexto de entrada, não de tokens de saída.

q: An agent occasionally processes international transfers without required compliance checks. The compliance team requires 100% enforcement of anti-money laundering (AML) checks before any international transfer. What is the correct approach?
opts: Add detailed AML check instructions to the system prompt with examples of correct behaviour
Implement a PreToolUse hook that blocks transfer execution until AML verification returns a pass result
Add a PostToolUse hook to flag completed transfers that skipped AML checks
Train the agent with few-shot examples showing the correct AML verification workflow
rat: 100% de enforcement nunca vem de prompt (A/D, probabilísticos) nem de detecção após o fato (C, tarde demais para AML). PreToolUse hook bloqueia a execução da transferência até a verificação AML passar — garantia no nível do cliente.

q: An architect is designing a customer support system using the Claude Agent SDK. The system needs a coordinator agent that can delegate to a billing specialist and a technical support specialist. Each specialist needs different tools. How should the architect configure the AgentDefinitions?
opts: Create one AgentDefinition with all tools from both specialists, and use prompt instructions to tell the agent which tools to use in each context
Create separate AgentDefinitions for each specialist with scoped tools (4-5 tools each), and give the coordinator the Task tool to spawn them
Create the specialists as separate API endpoints and have the coordinator call them via HTTP rather than the Task tool
Give the coordinator all tools and have it handle all requests directly, eliminating the need for subagents
rat: Padrão canônico do Agent SDK: AgentDefinitions separadas por especialista, cada uma com ferramentas escopadas (4–5), e o coordenador com a ferramenta Task para spawná-los. Um agente único com todas as tools (A/D) dilui seleção e viola o menor privilégio.

q: An engineer asks the agent to find all callers of a function before removing it. The function is defined in a core library but is also exposed through wrapper modules that rename the function for domain-specific use (e.g., calculateTax in the library becomes computeOrderTax in the orders module). What exploration strategy will most reliably identify all callers?
opts: Read the library and wrapper modules to identify all exposed names for the function, then Grep for each name across the codebase.
Use Grep to find all files that import from the library or wrapper modules, then read each file to check whether it uses the function.
Use Grep to search for the function's original name across the codebase.
Search for the function name in project documentation to understand intended usage patterns and navigate to documented integration points.
rat: Como os wrappers renomeiam a função, é preciso primeiro enumerar todos os nomes expostos (originais e aliases) e depois fazer Grep por cada um — só assim a cobertura é completa. Buscar só o nome original (C) perde os callers via wrappers; buscar imports (B) perde re-exports e usos indiretos; documentação (D) não reflete necessariamente o uso real do código.

q: An engineer asks the agent to understand how the caching layer works before adding a new cache invalidation trigger. After initial Grep searches, the agent has identified that caching logic spans 15 files including decorators, middleware, and service classes (~8,000 lines total). What's the most effective next step for building understanding while managing context constraints?
opts: Use the Read tool to sequentially load all 15 files, building complete understanding across the full caching implementation.
Analyze imports and class hierarchies to identify the base cache class, Read that file to understand the interface, then trace specific invalidation implementations.
Use Grep to search for "invalidate" and "expire" patterns across all files, then Read only those specific line ranges with minimal surrounding context.
Use Glob to find files matching common caching patterns (cache.py, caching/), prioritize the largest files by reading them first, then check smaller files for gaps.
rat: Identificar a classe base do cache e ler sua interface cria um modelo mental compacto que orienta o rastreio das implementações de invalidação — leitura direcionada em vez de exaustiva. Ler os 15 arquivos (A) consome ~8.000 linhas de contexto; grep por palavras-chave com contexto mínimo (C) dá fragmentos sem estrutura; priorizar por tamanho de arquivo (D) não corresponde a importância arquitetural.

q: An engineer asks your agent to identify untested code paths in a legacy payment processing module spanning 45 files. After reading the first 8 source files, the agent's responses are becoming noticeably less accurate—it's forgetting previously discussed code patterns and hasn't yet located all test files or traced critical payment flows. What's the most effective approach to complete this investigation?
opts: Document all current findings in a summary report, clear context completely, then use that report as the sole reference for continuing the investigation.

Spawn subagents to investigate specific questions (e.g., "find all test files for payment processing", "trace refund flow dependencies") while the main agent coordinates findings and preserves high-level understanding.
Clear context with /clear, then selectively re-read only the most critical files discovered so far, writing key findings to a scratchpad file that persists between context resets.
Switch to using Grep to search for specific function names instead of reading full files, reducing the content loaded into context for remaining exploration.
rat: Subagentes com contextos próprios investigam perguntas específicas e devolvem apenas conclusões, preservando o contexto do agente principal para coordenação e síntese — o padrão recomendado para exploração de bases grandes. Relatório + limpar tudo (A) perde nuance e continuidade; /clear com releitura seletiva (C) descarta entendimento já construído; só Grep (D) sacrifica profundidade de análise.

q: An engineer used `Claude Code` yesterday to investigate authentication flows in a legacy monolith, building up significant context over a 2-hour session. Today she wants to continue that specific investigation. She's worked on three other codebases since then and knows the session was named "auth-deep-dive". How should she resume?
opts: Start fresh and re-read the same files
Use `--session-id` with the UUID from yesterday's session transcript file
Use `--continue` to pick up where the most recent conversation left off
Use `--resume` auth-deep-dive to load that specific session by name
rat: O Claude Code permite retomar uma sessão específica com --resume seguido do identificador/nome da sessão, exatamente o caso de uma sessão nomeada 'auth-deep-dive' entre várias outras. Recomeçar do zero (A) desperdiça 2 horas de contexto construído, --session-id (B) serve para definir o ID de uma sessão nova, e --continue (C) retomaria a conversa mais recente, que é de outro codebase.

q: An engineer used the agent yesterday to analyze a legacy authentication module, identifying two distinct refactoring approaches: extracting a microservice versus refactoring in-place. Today, they want to explore both approaches in depth—having the agent propose specific code changes for each—before deciding which to implement. What's the most effective way to structure this exploration?
opts: Resume yesterday's session to explore the first approach, then start a new session for the second, manually recreating the original context.
Start two fresh sessions, manually providing a summary of yesterday's analysis findings to establish context.
Resume yesterday's session and explore both approaches sequentially within the same conversation thread.
Use fork_session to create two branches from yesterday's analysis, exploring one approach in each fork.
rat: fork_session cria duas ramificações a partir da mesma análise, herdando todo o contexto comum e mantendo cada exploração isolada — exatamente o caso de uso de forks. Recriar contexto manualmente (A) ou por resumo (B) perde fidelidade e duplica trabalho; explorar sequencialmente na mesma thread (C) contamina uma alternativa com o raciocínio da outra.

q: An engineer who just joined the team asks the agent to help them understand the authentication and authorization architecture before making security improvements. The codebase has 800+ files across multiple services. What exploration strategy will most effectively build understanding, given Claude built-in tools and context limits?
